basic_details.job_title: NHIDCL Associate Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 100 Posts
basic_details.post_name: Associate (under State Specific Graduate Scheme – SSGS)
basic_details.short_information: NHIDCL Associate Recruitment 2026 has been announced through Hiring Notice No. 07/2026 (F.No.269282/2163 dated 6 July 2026) by the National Highways and Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited (NHIDCL), inviting applications for 100 Associate posts under the State Specific Graduate Scheme (SSGS) across 14 States/UTs where NHIDCL operates. Eligible candidates can apply through the NHIDCL Resource Pool Portal from 8 July 2026 (10:00 AM) to 5 August 2026 (5:00 PM).

application_fee.details: The Hiring Notice does not specify any application fee for the Associate (SSGS) post; candidates should refer to the NHIDCL Resource Pool Portal for confirmation at the time of application.

age_limit.age_details: Purely contractual, as an independent consultant (not a staff member/official/employee of NHIDCL)

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: Graduation in Civil Engineering or an equivalent discipline from IITs, NITs, or other institutions ranked within the top 100 in the Engineering category of the NIRF Rankings for the year of graduation, or the preceding year in case the candidate is completing their graduation

qualification.details: Associate: Graduation in Civil Engineering or an equivalent discipline from IITs, NITs, or other institutions ranked within the top 100 in the Engineering category of the NIRF Rankings for the year of graduation, or the preceding year in case the candidate is completing their graduation

salary_details.details: The remuneration to be paid to the Associate(s), in the form of consultancy fees, shall be approved by the Competent Authority. The remuneration (consultancy fee) is inclusive of all applicable taxes, and no other facility or allowance is admissible, except that Associates deployed in any Regional Office (RO), Project Monitoring Unit (PMU), or Site Office (SO) located in the Andaman & Nicobar Islands shall be entitled to a Location Allowance at the rate of 20% of the prescribed consultancy fee, payable for the period of actual deployment, subject to NHIDCL’s terms and conditions. An annual enhancement of 5% in the consultancy fee shall be allowed. Monthly remuneration will be paid within 7 days after completion of the month, subject to periodic completion of work certified by the Controlling Officer. Income tax or any other applicable tax will be deducted at source as per prevailing rules, for which NHIDCL will issue TDS certificates. Individual consultants are liable to pay Goods and Services Tax (GST), as applicable. NHIDCL undertakes no liability for taxes or other contributions payable by the Associates on payments made under the contract.

selection_process: The Hiring Notice directs eligible candidates to apply through the NHIDCL Resource Pool Portal; the notice does not detail a separate written test
and selection proceeds through NHIDCL shortlisting candidates from the Eligible Pool of Applicants for interview
as decided by the Competent Authority based on vacancy availability and operational requirements.
how_to_apply: Eligible candidates may apply through the NHIDCL Resource Pool Portal.
Visit the Resource Pool Portal – Go to Apply Here.

Register/Update Profile – Register yourself and submit your application/profile for the relevant position and State/UT, ensuring you meet the domicile, qualification, and age criteria for that position.
Submit Within the Window – Applications must be submitted between 8 July 2026, 10:00 AM, and 5 August 2026, 5:00 PM.

Interested candidates may register themselves on the Resource Pool Portal and update their profile at any time during the year, even if the last date of application for a particular advertisement has already passed. NHIDCL may also issue abridged advertisements directing prospective candidates to register and submit their profiles through the Resource Pool Portal for future requirements.
All correspondence shall be made only via email at [email protected]. Any other mode of correspondence shall not be valid.
important_instructions: Candidates must ensure they meet all eligibility criteria and possess essential qualification recognized by AICTE/UGC/State Technical Boards. Furnishing inaccurate, wrong, incomplete, or misleading information may lead to rejection of the candidature. Candidates must keep their email ID active for one year; no change in the email ID would be allowed. Corrigendum, addendum, or errata will be posted only on NHIDCL's website at www.nhidcl.com, under the heading "Resource Portal". Canvassing or external influence will disqualify the candidate from selection. Candidates should avoid middlemen, recruitment agents/consultants, and fraudulent job promises. All disputes are subject to Delhi High Court jurisdiction, with the English version prevailing for interpretation. Personal information collected during the verification process shall be handled in accordance with applicable laws, rules, and NHIDCL's policies, and used only for the purpose of assessment and verification relevant to the engagement. For General Terms and Conditions of Engagement, candidates should refer to Section 5 of the Scheme for Young Professionals, Expert Professionals, and Empanelment of Persons of Eminence. Provisions of the Scheme shall apply and supersede any contradictory instructions in this Recruitment Notice.

rows.details: National Highways and Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited (NHIDCL)
Associate (under State Specific Graduate Scheme – SSGS)
Purely contractual, as an independent consultant (not a staff member/official/employee of NHIDCL)
Fixed period of 2 years, extendable by 1 year at a time up to a maximum of 3 years (2+1)
Online, through the NHIDCL Resource Pool Portal

content_sections.heading: NHIDCL Associate Recruitment 2026 Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates are advised to regularly check the Resource Pool Portal for updates regarding shortlisting, interview schedules, and other communications, and to watch www.nhidcl.com under the "Resource Portal" heading for any corrigendum, addendum, or errata.

content_sections.heading: State/UT-Wise Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: NHIDCL Associate Recruitment 2026 covers 100 vacancies distributed across 14 States and Union Territories where NHIDCL operates. Special Provision for Project-Specific Engagements: Notwithstanding the above, where requirements arise for specific projects, Project Management Units (PMUs), Site Offices, or other field offices, particularly in remote, border, strategic, hilly, or difficult terrain locations, NHIDCL may, with the approval of the Competent Authority, engage suitable candidates from the Eligible Pool of Applicants of the concerned State/UT. Such engagements may be made against existing or future vacancies under the Scheme and shall be subject to terms and conditions specified by NHIDCL. Note: The number of vacancies indicated is tentative and may increase or decrease depending on project requirements, organizational needs, and availability of work, as decided by the Competent Authority. NHIDCL reserves the right to fill all, some, or none of the advertised vacancies without assigning any reason.

rows.qualification: Graduation in Civil Engineering or an equivalent discipline from IITs, NITs, or other institutions ranked within the top 100 in the Engineering category of the NIRF Rankings for the year of graduation, or the preceding year in case the candidate is completing their graduation

content_sections.heading: Remuneration (Consultancy Fee)
content_sections.text: The remuneration to be paid to the Associate(s), in the form of consultancy fees, shall be approved by the Competent Authority. The remuneration (consultancy fee) is inclusive of all applicable taxes, and no other facility or allowance is admissible, except that Associates deployed in any Regional Office (RO), Project Monitoring Unit (PMU), or Site Office (SO) located in the Andaman & Nicobar Islands shall be entitled to a Location Allowance at the rate of 20% of the prescribed consultancy fee, payable for the period of actual deployment, subject to NHIDCL’s terms and conditions. An annual enhancement of 5% in the consultancy fee shall be allowed. Monthly remuneration will be paid within 7 days after completion of the month, subject to periodic completion of work certified by the Controlling Officer. Income tax or any other applicable tax will be deducted at source as per prevailing rules, for which NHIDCL will issue TDS certificates. Individual consultants are liable to pay Goods and Services Tax (GST), as applicable. NHIDCL undertakes no liability for taxes or other contributions payable by the Associates on payments made under the contract.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The Hiring Notice does not specify any application fee for the Associate (SSGS) post; candidates should refer to the NHIDCL Resource Pool Portal for confirmation at the time of application.

content_sections.heading: Tenure & Type of Engagement
content_sections.text: The engagement is at 1 (one) level – Associate. The engagement is purely on a contractual basis . Associates have the legal status of an independent consultant vis-à-vis NHIDCL and shall not be regarded, for any purpose, as a staff member, official, or employee of NHIDCL. Nothing in the individual consultancy contracts or these guidelines establishes an employer-employee or principal-agent relationship between NHIDCL and the Associates. Associates will be engaged for a fixed period of 2 (two) years , which may be extended by 1 (one) year at a time up to a maximum tenure of 3 (three) years , i.e., 2+1 years, for providing high-quality services on specific projects as per the requirement of the verticals/divisions. Continuation beyond the first and subsequent years is contingent on the decision of the Consultants’ Review Committee (CRC).

content_sections.heading: Terms of Reference (Role & Responsibilities)
content_sections.text: The Terms of Reference (ToR) include the outputs to be delivered and the functions to be performed, which shall be specific, measurable, attainable, results-based, and time-bound. An indicative ToR for Associates (SSGS) is as follows; detailed ToR will be drawn up by the respective Regional Offices of NHIDCL for which Associates are selected, and this ToR is deemed part of the contract. Project Preparation & Design Appraisal: Assist in the review of feasibility studies, DPRs, alignment plans, and compliance with IRC/MoRTH standards. Construction Supervision & Project Monitoring: Undertake field visits, monitor physical/financial progress, and report on quality, safety, and adherence to specifications. Contract & Documentation Support: Assist in contract management, correspondence, technical notes, and maintenance of project records/MIS. Quality Assurance & Technical Compliance: Review test results, site practices, and ensure compliance with approved drawings and Quality Assurance Plans (QAPs). Post-Construction & O&M Support: Support completion documentation, punch-list closure, and monitoring during the defect liability period.

content_sections.heading: Induction & Payment
content_sections.text: There will be an Induction Module of 3 (three) days , which each hired Associate must go through. The Competent Authority may prescribe "Competency Tests" at any time, periodic or otherwise, and Associates are expected to clear these as per the minimum qualifying marks prescribed. All engagements are subject to the successful completion of a Background Verification (BGV) process , which may include verification of the candidate's identity, address, educational and professional qualifications, character antecedents, and any other information deemed necessary by NHIDCL. If the outcome of the BGV is found unsatisfactory at any point – before or after appointment – NHIDCL reserves the right to withdraw or cancel the engagement.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: The Hiring Notice directs eligible candidates to apply through the NHIDCL Resource Pool Portal; the notice does not detail a separate written test, and selection proceeds through NHIDCL shortlisting candidates from the Eligible Pool of Applicants for interview, assessment, or other stages, as decided by the Competent Authority based on vacancy availability and operational requirements.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online for NHIDCL Associate Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Eligible candidates may apply through the NHIDCL Resource Pool Portal. Visit the Resource Pool Portal – Go to Apply Here . Register/Update Profile – Register yourself and submit your application/profile for the relevant position and State/UT, ensuring you meet the domicile, qualification, and age criteria for that position. Submit Within the Window – Applications must be submitted between 8 July 2026, 10:00 AM, and 5 August 2026, 5:00 PM . All correspondence shall be made only via email at [email protected] . Any other mode of correspondence shall not be valid.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Candidates must ensure they meet all eligibility criteria and possess essential qualification recognized by AICTE/UGC/State Technical Boards. Furnishing inaccurate, wrong, incomplete, or misleading information may lead to rejection of the candidature. Candidates must keep their email ID active for one year; no change in the email ID would be allowed. Corrigendum, addendum, or errata will be posted only on NHIDCL's website at www.nhidcl.com , under the heading "Resource Portal". Canvassing or external influence will disqualify the candidate from selection. Candidates should avoid middlemen, recruitment agents/consultants, and fraudulent job promises. All disputes are subject to Delhi High Court jurisdiction, with the English version prevailing for interpretation. Personal information collected during the verification process shall be handled in accordance with applicable laws, rules, and NHIDCL's policies, and used only for the purpose of assessment and verification relevant to the engagement. For General Terms and Conditions of Engagement, candidates should refer to Section 5 of the Scheme for Young Professionals, Expert Professionals, and Empanelment of Persons of Eminence. Provisions of the Scheme shall apply and supersede any contradictory instructions in this Recruitment Notice.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in NHIDCL Associate Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 100 vacancies for the Associate post under the State Specific Graduate Scheme (SSGS), spread across 14 States/UTs where NHIDCL operates.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for NHIDCL Associate Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application window closes on 5 August 2026 at 5:00 PM. Applications opened on 8 July 2026 at 10:00 AM.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for the NHIDCL Associate post?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 30 years, as on the date of issue of the Hiring Notice.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary for NHIDCL Associate (SSGS)?
faq.answer: Associates are paid a monthly consultancy fee of Rs 70,000 to Rs 80,000, inclusive of all applicable taxes, with an annual enhancement of 5%. Associates deployed in the Andaman & Nicobar Islands additionally get a 20% Location Allowance.

faq.question: Q5. What is the tenure of engagement for NHIDCL Associates?
faq.answer: The engagement is for a fixed period of 2 years, extendable by 1 year at a time up to a maximum of 3 years, subject to the decision of the Consultants’ Review Committee.

faq.question: Q6. Can candidates from any state apply for any state's vacancies?
faq.answer: No, candidates can only apply for vacancies advertised for their own State/UT of domicile, except for Andaman & Nicobar Islands vacancies, which are open to candidates from other States/UTs as well, though domicile candidates are given preference.

faq.question: Q7. What qualification is required for the NHIDCL Associate post?
faq.answer: Candidates need Graduation in Civil Engineering or an equivalent discipline from IITs, NITs, or institutions ranked in the top 100 of the NIRF Engineering Rankings for their year of graduation (or the preceding year, if completing graduation), along with 0 to 2 years of relevant experience.

faq.question: Q8. How can I apply for NHIDCL Associate Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Eligible candidates must apply through the NHIDCL Resource Pool Portal at nhidcl.com/application/login. Candidates who already applied under Hiring Notice No. 05/2025 and remain in the Eligible Pool of Applicants need not apply again.

faq.question: Q9. Is NHIDCL Associate a permanent government job?
faq.answer: No, the engagement is purely contractual, and Associates have the legal status of an independent consultant. They are not regarded as staff members, officials, or employees of NHIDCL for any purpose.
